metadata.title: Bytes to KB converter – convert B to KB
metadata.description: Convert bytes to kilobytes and back. 1 kilobyte is 1,024 bytes in the binary system. Type a value and the result appears instantly. Runs in your browser with no sign-up.

hero.title: Convert bytes to kilobytes
hero.description: Type a number of bytes and read the result in kilobytes straight away. A kilobyte is 1,024 bytes in the binary system, so the converter divides your value by 1,024. Use Swap order to convert kilobytes to bytes instead.

software.name: Bytes to kilobytes converter – B to KB calculator
alternateName: How many bytes in a kilobyte
software.description: Bytes to kilobytes converter that runs in your browser with no sign-up. Type a number of bytes and read the result in kilobytes instantly. It uses the binary system, where 1 KB is 1,024 bytes.
featureList: Converts bytes to kilobytes and kilobytes to bytes in the binary system (1 KB = 1,024 B)
The conversion works both ways — the Swap order button switches the direction
The result appears instantly as you type, with no button to click
The Copy result button puts the finished value on your clipboard
Accepts both a dot and a comma as the decimal separator
Every calculation runs locally in your browser, so your data never leaves your device
The exact formula is: kilobytes = bytes ÷ 1,024
Works on phone, tablet and computer with no installation

howTo.name: How do you convert bytes to kilobytes?
howTo.description: A short guide to using the bytes to kilobytes converter.

steps.name: Type a number of bytes
steps.text: In the left field, type the number of bytes you want to convert to kilobytes. You can use a dot or a comma as the decimal separator.

steps.name: Read the result in kilobytes
steps.text: The result in kilobytes appears immediately in the field on the right. The Copy result button puts it on your clipboard.

steps.name: Swap the direction if you need to
steps.text: Click Swap order to move to converting kilobytes to bytes.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert bytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.html:
A byte is the basic unit of data size, and a kilobyte is 1,024 bytes. Raw sizes in bytes show up in file properties, in the output of system commands and in API responses — converting them to kilobytes makes them easier to read and compare.

The relationship is fixed: 1 KB is 1,024 B in the binary system, which operating systems and this converter use. To turn bytes into kilobytes, divide their number by 1,024. The converter does this the moment you type a value.

Watch out for two systems. In the decimal system (SI), 1 KB is 1,000 B — that is how storage makers count, for example. This converter uses the binary system (1,024 B), matching what Windows and macOS show. Do not mix bytes up with bits either: 1 byte is 8 bits.
contentBlocks.imageAlt: Free bytes to kilobytes converter – Arteon

contentBlocks.title: How does the bytes to kilobytes conversion work? Formula and example
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion rests on a fixed relationship: one kilobyte is 1,024 bytes. To turn bytes into kilobytes, divide their number by 1,024.

Full formula: kilobytes = bytes ÷ 1,024. The other way: bytes = kilobytes × 1,024.

Example: a file is 51,200 bytes. Calculation: 51,200 ÷ 1,024 = 50 KB. The multiplier 1,024 comes from the fact that computers count in binary, and two to the power of ten is exactly 1,024.

Why 1,024 and not 1,000? In the decimal system (SI), 1 KB is 1,000 B, and that is how storage makers state capacity. Operating systems and this converter use the binary system, where 1 KB is 1,024 B — formally called a kibibyte (KiB).

contentBlocks.caption: Example: 1,024 B ÷ 1,024 = 1 KB. Want to convert kilobytes to bytes? Click Swap order in the converter above.

contentBlocks.title: When do people most often convert bytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.description: Sizes in bytes come up mostly in technical contexts.

items.title: Reading a file size
items.description: File properties, system commands (such as ls -l) and file managers often report a size in raw bytes. A 51,200 B file is 50 KB — once converted, you know at a glance how big it really is.

items.title: Programming and APIs
items.description: API responses, the Content-Length header and upload limits are given in bytes. Converting to kilobytes makes it easier to judge whether a 20,480 B (20 KB) response fits your data budget.

items.title: Small files on a website
items.description: Favicons, SVG icons, JSON and CSS files are measured in kilobytes, but tools report them in bytes. Converting helps you keep an eye on asset weight, which affects how fast the site loads.

items.title: Learning computing
items.description: The byte–kilobyte relationship is the basis of many computing exercises. The converter shows plainly that 1 KB is 1,024 B and lets you check your own calculations quickly.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about converting bytes to kilobytes

items.question: How many bytes are in 1 kilobyte?
items.answer: 1 kilobyte is 1,024 bytes in the binary system, which this converter and operating systems use. In the decimal system (SI), used by storage makers among others, 1 KB is 1,000 bytes.

items.question: How much is 1024 bytes in KB?
items.answer: 1,024 bytes is exactly 1 KB. Calculation: 1,024 ÷ 1,024 = 1 KB. This is the base relationship between the byte and the kilobyte, which is why it is looked up most often.

items.question: How do you convert bytes to kilobytes?
items.answer: Divide the number of bytes by 1,024. For example, 51,200 B ÷ 1,024 = 50 KB. The converter does this the moment you type a value, with no button to click.

items.question: How do you convert kilobytes to bytes?
items.answer: Multiply the number of kilobytes by 1,024. For example, 10 KB × 1,024 = 10,240 B. Click Swap order to have the converter work in this direction.

items.question: Is 1 KB 1,000 or 1,024 bytes?
items.answer: It depends on the system. Operating systems and this converter use the binary system, where 1 KB is 1,024 B. Storage makers use the decimal system, where 1 KB is 1,000 B. That is why the same number of bytes can give a slightly different result in KB.

items.question: What is the difference between KB and KiB?
items.answer: KiB (kibibyte, the IEC standard) is always exactly 1,024 B. KB in the SI system is 1,000 B. In everyday use most people write KB while meaning 1,024 B, which is technically KiB. This converter uses 1,024 B.

items.question: How many bits are in a byte?
items.answer: 1 byte is 8 bits. Mind the notation: KB means kilobytes (file size), while Kb means kilobits (often transfer speed). This tool works with bytes and kilobytes, not bits.

contentBlocks.title: Have a question or suggestion?
contentBlocks.description: If you have spotted an error in the table or have an idea to improve the converter, write to us. We reply to every message.
